consistency.name: Consistent with standard D&D 5e formatting.
consistency.level: Level 3 spell, consistent with school and typical power level.
consistency.school: Necromancy, appropriate for a life-draining spell.
consistency.range: Melee touch attack, consistent with 'touch' in the name.
consistency.duration: Concentration up to 1 minute, standard for ongoing combat effects.
consistency.classes: Warlock and Wizard are standard users of Necromancy spells.

quality.description: Clear and mechanically precise. The scaling for higher levels is clearly defined.
quality.mechanics: The core loop (Melee Attack -> Damage -> Self-Heal) is standard for life-leech mechanics. Allowing a repeat attack on subsequent turns using an action is a significant resource commitment, balancing the healing/damage output.
quality.scalability: Scaling adds 1d6 damage per slot level above 3rd. This is a clear and balanced scaling method for Necromancy damage spells.

mechanics_review.core_interaction: Spell requires a successful melee spell attack, not a saving throw, which is common for touch spells.
mechanics_review.healing_ratio: Healing equals exactly half the necrotic damage dealt (e.g., 3d6 damage = 1.5d6 HP regained), which is a favorable but not overpowered ratio for a combat spell that costs an action to repeat.
mechanics_review.action_cost_of_repeat: The requirement to use a full action every turn after the initial casting to continue dealing damage/healing is the primary balancing factor for its sustained power.
mechanics_review.damage_type: Necrotic damage is consistent with the school.
